# Fuzzing Triage Report

## Crash analysis
No crash or DoS was observed: every frame across all three families and six mutations reported alive=1, and the per-frame table is the same 18-row block repeated ~1374 times, so the campaign exercised only 18 unique stimuli at high redundancy. The target's management-frame parser tolerated oversized IE values (beacon 348B, auth 287B, assoc 327B), duplicate IEs, reserved element IDs, and missing mandatory elements without faulting. Critically, the run is partly under-powered by a generator defect: len_overflow is a no-op for all three families (beacon 101=101, authentication 48=48, assoc_request 80=80 — identical to baseline), so the 'malformed IE length field' class was never actually delivered on the wire. For authentication this is expected (Open System carries no IEs to overflow), but for beacon and assoc_request it indicates the mutation failed to corrupt an IE length octet (consistent with the prior 'empty-value no-op' fix not covering the length-overflow path). The most security-relevant length-boundary class is therefore effectively untested, which is the leading reason nothing crashed rather than evidence the target is robust against it.

## Findings
- [anomaly] beacon/len_overflow: beacon/len_overflow len=101 is identical to beacon/baseline len=101 — the len_overflow mutation produced a byte-for-byte unchanged frame, so no malformed IE length field was actually exercised. This is a generator bug, not a target behavior; the mutation contributed zero coverage across all ~4123 reps.
- [anomaly] authentication/len_overflow: authentication/len_overflow len=48 equals authentication/baseline len=48. Authentication (Open System) carries no IEs, so an IE-length-overflow mutation has nothing to target and silently degrades to baseline. Wasted reps with no distinct stimulus.
- [anomaly] assoc_request/len_overflow: assoc_request/len_overflow len=80 equals assoc_request/baseline len=80 — same no-op as the other families. The overflow mutation never altered an IE length octet, confirming the issue is systemic to len_overflow rather than per-family.
- [info] beacon/oversized_value: beacon/oversized_value len=348 (vs 101 baseline) is the largest stimulus exercised and the target stayed alive=1. Confirms the AP parser tolerates a single grossly oversized IE value within a Beacon without crashing.
- [info] authentication/oversized_value: authentication/oversized_value len=287 stayed alive. Notable because Open System auth has no IEs in spec, so this injects an unexpected trailing/oversized element into a fixed-field-only frame; parser ignored or safely discarded it.
- [info] assoc_request/missing_mandatory: assoc_request/missing_mandatory len=70 (vs 80 baseline) drops a mandatory element (SSID or Supported Rates) yet target stayed alive. The AP rejects/handles the malformed Association Request gracefully rather than faulting.
- [info] beacon/dup_ie + beacon/reserved_ie: Duplicate-IE (len=111) and reserved-element-ID (len=111) Beacon variants both stayed alive across all reps. Parser tolerates duplicated and unknown/reserved element IDs without state corruption.
- [info] campaign/no-crash: All 24738 replayed frames returned alive=1; target never stopped responding. With ~16x repetition of only 18 distinct (family×mutation) vectors, coverage breadth is the limiting factor, not target fragility.

## Next plan
{
  "focus_frames": [
    "beacon",
    "assoc_request"
  ],
  "focus_mutations": [
    "oversized_value",
    "len_overflow",
    "missing_mandatory",
    "dup_ie"
  ],
  "drop_frames": [
    "authentication"
  ],
  "rationale": "First fix the len_overflow generator so it emits a real declared-length-vs-actual mismatch (declared length > remaining buffer, and the 255-byte max) on a concrete IE \u2014 beacon SSID(0)/Supported Rates(1)/RSN(48) and assoc_request RSN(48)/Extended Capabilities(127) \u2014 since this boundary class was never delivered and is where parser overruns typically hide; pair it with oversized_value which is the only large-frame stimulus confirmed to reach the parser. Concentrate on beacon and assoc_request because they are IE-rich and accept many optional/conditional elements (DS, TIM, Country, RSN, Ext Rates, Ext Caps), giving real surface for dup_ie and missing_mandatory permutations; vary which mandatory element is dropped and which IE is duplicated rather than repeating one vector. Drop authentication: Open System has no IEs, so len_overflow/dup_ie/reserved_ie/missing_mandatory/oversized_value have no meaningful target there \u2014 instead its budget should later go to fuzzing the fixed fields (algorithm number incl. Shared Key=1/SAE=3, transaction sequence, status code) and Shared-Key Challenge Text (IE 16), which the current mutation set does not touch. Also cut the ~16x blind repetition of identical frames; spend that budget on new distinct vectors and on confirming any future crash with a tight replay loop."
}

## Raw campaign summary
Total frames replayed: 24738
Frame families exercised: assoc_request=8244, authentication=8244, beacon=8250
Mutations exercised: baseline=4123, dup_ie=4123, len_overflow=4123, missing_mandatory=4123, oversized_value=4123, reserved_ie=4123
No crash observed (target stayed alive for all frames).
